description: Mandatory rules to ensure the application is usable by people with the widest possible range of abilities.
philosophy: Build for everyone, or build for no one. Accessibility is a core requirement, not an afterthought.

rules.name: Semantic HTML Usage
rules.description: Use HTML elements for their intended purpose. Do not use `div` for everything.
guidelines: Use `<button>` for clickable actions, not `<div onClick={...}>`.
Use heading tags (`<h1>`, `<h2>`, etc.) in a logical order.

rules.name: Keyboard Navigability
rules.description: All interactive elements (links, buttons, inputs) MUST be focusable and operable using only the keyboard.
rules.implementation: Ensure `outline: none` is never used without providing a visible focus alternative (e.g., a ring or box-shadow).

rules.name: Image Accessibility
rules.description: All `` elements MUST have a descriptive `alt` attribute.
guidelines: If the image is purely decorative, use `alt=""`.
If the image conveys information, the `alt` text must describe that information.

rules.name: ARIA for Dynamic Components
rules.description: Use ARIA (Accessible Rich Internet Applications) attributes to provide context for dynamic components.
